project.objective: Identify the parents of Patrick Flynn, born ca. 1845 in Pennsylvania, died 1908 in Schuylkill County, PA

questions.question: Who were the parents of Patrick Flynn (b. ~1845, PA, d. 1908)?
questions.rationale: This is the primary research objective.

exhaustive_declaration.justification: All planned direct record types searched: 1850 census (FamilySearch, Ancestry, MyHeritage), 1860 census (FamilySearch), death certificate (FamilySearch), 1870/1880/1900 census (FamilySearch — all negative), probate Schuylkill County 1870–1900 (FamilySearch — negative), Catholic church records Schuylkill County 1840–1870 (FamilySearch — negative). No further direct record types remain unsearched for this question. FAN research is the only avenue not yet explored.

stop_criteria.goal_alignment: Partially — Thomas Flynn is a strong candidate as father (three independent source types) but not proved. Exhaustive search has been conducted without producing a definitive record.
stop_criteria.repository_breadth: Census (FamilySearch, Ancestry, MyHeritage), vital records (FamilySearch), probate (FamilySearch), church records (FamilySearch). Major repositories and record types searched.
stop_criteria.original_substitution: FamilySearch provides original images for census and death certificate records.
stop_criteria.independent_verification: FamilySearch and Ancestry both confirm the 1850 census household. Three record types independently support Thomas Flynn as father.
stop_criteria.evidence_class: Three original source types; all relationship evidence is indirect or secondary.
stop_criteria.overturn_risk: Low for Thomas Flynn as candidate. No competing candidates found.

questions.question: Where was Patrick Flynn in the 1850 census?
questions.rationale: The 1850 census is the earliest enumeration where Patrick would appear by name (age ~5). Locating him in a household identifies candidate parents.

exhaustive_declaration.justification: Searched 1850 census for Schuylkill County on FamilySearch (indexed and browse), Ancestry (indexed), and MyHeritage (indexed). All three returned the same household. No other Patrick Flynn of matching age found in the county.

stop_criteria.goal_alignment: Yes — Patrick Flynn located in a specific 1850 household, answering the question.
stop_criteria.repository_breadth: Three major repositories searched (FamilySearch, Ancestry, MyHeritage). No additional known indexes of the 1850 Schuylkill County census exist.
stop_criteria.original_substitution: FamilySearch provides the original census image; Ancestry index is derivative but confirmed consistent.
stop_criteria.independent_verification: FamilySearch (original) and Ancestry (derivative) both return the same household. Two access paths, one underlying original.
stop_criteria.evidence_class: Yes — FamilySearch provides original census image with indeterminate-quality information.
stop_criteria.conflict_resolution: No conflicts on the 1850 census placement question.
stop_criteria.overturn_risk: Low — all three repositories agree, and no competing Patrick Flynn of matching age exists in the county.

items.rationale: 1850 census fully indexed on FamilySearch. Free access, start here.
Ancestry has independent indexing; cross-check for transcription errors.
Third independent index for coverage confirmation.
Confirm Patrick still in Thomas Flynn household in 1860.
Death certificate may name parents directly.
Search 1870 census to locate Patrick or Thomas Flynn post-1860. Would corroborate the household relationship into adulthood.
1880 census names relationships explicitly — would provide direct relationship evidence if Patrick is still in Thomas's household.
Search 1900 census for Patrick Flynn in Schuylkill County — may list parents' birthplaces.
Thomas Flynn's will or probate inventory would name his children directly, providing primary-quality evidence for the parentage hypothesis.
Catholic baptism and marriage registers for Irish immigrants in Schuylkill County may list Thomas Flynn's children.

log.notes: Found Patrick Flynn age 5 in household of Thomas Flynn, dwelling 84, Schuylkill Co. Three other Flynn results examined — ages and locations don't match.
Ancestry index confirms same household. Transcription matches FamilySearch.
MyHeritage returned no results for Patrick Flynn in 1850 Schuylkill County.
Patrick Flynn age 15 in household of Thomas Flynn, Schuylkill Co. Consistent with 1850 placement.
Death certificate found. Names Thomas Flynn as father. Informant was son-in-law James Brown.
No Patrick Flynn of matching age in Schuylkill County or adjacent counties (Carbon, Lebanon, Northumberland, Dauphin). Two Pennsylvania Patrick Flynns examined — neither matches on age or birthplace.
No Patrick Flynn of matching profile in Schuylkill County. Broadened to all Pennsylvania — three candidates examined, none matches.
No Patrick Flynn matching profile in Pennsylvania. 1890 census was destroyed — that gap cannot be searched.

Searched Schuylkill County Register of Wills on FamilySearch for Thomas Flynn, 1870–1900. No probate record found. Thomas Flynn may have died intestate, died outside Schuylkill County, or his records did not survive.

Searched FamilySearch Catholic church records for Schuylkill County, 1840–1870 (St. Patrick's Pottsville, St. John's Minersville, St. Clair). No Thomas Flynn household record found. Three Flynn baptism records examined — children don't match Patrick's profile.

1850 U.S. Census, Schuylkill County, Pennsylvania, population schedule, dwelling 84, Thomas Flynn household; digital image, Ancestry.com, accessed 1 May 2026.

sources.notes: Ancestry's index of the same original census. Transcription consistent with FamilySearch.

citation_detail.who: Pennsylvania Department of Health; informant: James Brown (son-in-law)

sources.notes: Informant is son-in-law James Brown. Primary for death facts, secondary for birth/parentage facts.

assertions.informant: Unknown informant (most likely a household member) reporting to census enumerator
assertions.informant_bias_notes: Census enumerator is the recorder, not the informant.

assertions.informant: Unknown — most likely a household member
assertions.informant_bias_notes: Age and birthplace require active reporting by an informant.

assertions.informant: Census enumerator (witness to residence by visiting the dwelling)
assertions.informant_bias_notes: For residence facts, the enumerator is a direct witness.

assertions.value: Listed in household of Thomas Flynn (head), position consistent with child
assertions.informant_bias_notes: 1850 census does not state relationships; inferred from household position and shared surname.

assertions.value: Listed in household of Thomas Flynn (head), position and age consistent with son

person_evidence.rationale: Name matches (Patrick Flynn), age consistent with ~1845 birth, located in Schuylkill County.
Household position and shared surname with head Thomas Flynn suggest parent-child relationship.
Patrick Flynn age 15 in Thomas Flynn household, 1860 census.
Death certificate for Patrick Flynn names Thomas Flynn as father.

conflicts.description: Patrick Flynn's birthplace: Ireland (1850 and 1860 censuses) vs. Pennsylvania (1908 death certificate)
conflicts.independence_analysis: The two census records are independent originals. The death certificate is a third independent original. However, census informants are likely the same household member.
conflicts.weighing_analysis: Census records are contemporary; death certificate is 63 years later with a secondary informant.
conflicts.resolution_rationale: Ireland accepted. Son-in-law may have confused birthplace with residence.

hypotheses.claim: Patrick Flynn's father was Thomas Flynn of Schuylkill County, Pennsylvania
hypotheses.notes: Three independent pieces of evidence support this hypothesis. All direct record types have been exhausted: 1850 and 1860 census (FamilySearch, Ancestry, MyHeritage), death certificate (FamilySearch), 1870/1880/1900 census (all negative), Schuylkill County probate 1870–1900 (negative), Catholic church records Schuylkill County 1840–1870 (negative). No additional direct record types remain unsearched. FAN research (neighbors, witnesses, land record co-signatories, church associates of Thomas Flynn) is the next available research avenue.

timelines.label: Patrick Flynn — assuming Thomas Flynn parentage

events.description: Born in Ireland, estimated from census ages
Enumerated age 5 in Thomas Flynn household, dwelling 84
Enumerated age 15 in Thomas Flynn household, dwelling 112
Died, death certificate names Thomas Flynn as father

gaps.notes: 1870, 1880, and 1900 censuses searched — Patrick Flynn not found. 1890 census destroyed. Remaining gap involves marriage, church, and occupation records not yet searched.

proof_summaries.exhaustive_search_summary: Searched 1850 census (FamilySearch, Ancestry, MyHeritage — log_001–log_003), 1860 census (FamilySearch — log_004), death certificate (FamilySearch — log_005), 1870 census (FamilySearch — log_006, negative), 1880 census (FamilySearch — log_007, negative), 1900 census (FamilySearch — log_008, negative). Patrick Flynn not found in post-1860 censuses. Direct evidence searches exhausted.
proof_summaries.narrative_markdown: Patrick Flynn is **Probably** the son of Thomas Flynn of Schuylkill County, Pennsylvania, based on co-enumeration in the 1850 and 1860 censuses and a death certificate naming Thomas as father. Conclusion remains at **Probable** because both census sources provide only indirect relationship evidence (relationship column not introduced until 1880), and the death certificate informant (son-in-law) is secondary for parentage facts. Direct census searches for 1870, 1880, and 1900 returned no Patrick Flynn of matching profile, leaving the post-1860 period undocumented. Associates/neighbors research has not yet been attempted.
